channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Why super-sized and politicised World Cup comes at a cost
item.description: Whatever one's perspective, it seems certain that away from the on-field spectacle, this super-sized World Cup could be among the most contentious ever.

item.title: Tickets for Iran fans revoked, says federation
item.description: Iran's football federation says its allocation of tickets for the group stage of the World Cup has been revoked just days before the tournament.

item.title: Play BBC Sport's new World Cup predictor game
item.description: Play BBC Sport's new World Cup predictor game to be in with the chance of winning a signed football.

item.title: 'Mazadona', 'Einstein', 'The Ant' - 20 new players to watch at the 2026 World Cup
item.description: BBC Sport TV and radio commentators pick the players they think could be the breakout stars of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: The flag Iranians are not allowed to wave at the World Cup
item.description: Iran football fans living in Los Angeles explain why they want to wave Iran's pre-1979 Islamic revolution flag and dismiss the one that is on their team's shirt.

item.title: Why France's Olise is moving into Ballon d'Or picture
item.description: After a first international hat-trick against Northern Ireland, former France striker Louis Saha believes Michael Olise can move into Ballon d'Or contention.

item.title: 'Diogo will be with you' - Jota's widow writes to Robertson
item.description: Diogo Jota's widow urges Scotland captain Andrew Robertson to carry his former Liverpool team-mate "in your heart" when he plays at the World Cup.

item.title: Wolves sign Trippier and in Jimenez talks
item.description: Wolves sign former England full-back Kieran Trippier and are in talks to re-sign former striker Raul Jimenez on a free transfer.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 2
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Palmer and Joao Pedro feature in Madonna video
item.description: Chelsea forwards Cole Palmer and Joao Pedro make a surprise appearance in a promotional film for Madonna's new album.

item.title: Platini begins legal proceedings against Fifa
item.description: Former Uefa president Michel Platini initiaites civil and legal proceedings against Fifa and current president Gianni Infantino over corruption allegations from 2015.

item.title: Somali referee Artan to miss World Cup after being barred from entering US
item.description: Omar Artan, who was set to be the first Somali to referee at the World Cup finals, is dropped from the list of officials after he was denied entry to the United States.

item.title: How to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

item.title: World Cup 2026: All 48 squads confirmed
item.description: All 48 confirmed squads for the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico.

item.title: Download your World Cup 2026 wallchart
item.description: Get your essential piece of kit now by downloading the Fifa World Cup 2026 wallchart.

item.title: Two brothers, two nations, one World Cup & how grief bound them
item.description: In July 2022, brothers Harry and John Souttar lost their hero, but as both prepare to represent different nations at the World Cup, his influence lives on.

item.title: Spurs close in on Man City's Savinho - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Spurs make progress with negotiations to sign Manchester City winger Savinho, Manchester United are battling their city rivals for Nottingham Forest midfielder Elliot Anderson, Atletico Madrid and Barcelona eye Chelsea full-back Marc Cucurella, plus more.

item.title: Football regulator contacts West Ham over 'serious allegations' against Sullivan
item.description: Multiple women accuse the billionaire of abusing his power and preying on them for sex, in some cases when they were in their late teens.

item.title: Scotland's fateful 1978 World Cup campaign still resonates
item.description: There's a mesmeric quality to this story of '78, a legend of how a man and a country lost the run of itself, writes Tom English.

item.title: Eriksen at home and 'doing well' after collapse
item.description: Christian Eriksen says he is "doing well" and is at home with his family following his collapse in Sunday's game against Ukraine.

item.title: Man City and Chelsea remain in talks over Maresca
item.description: Manchester City are continuing negotiations with Chelsea to appoint Enzo Maresca as their new manager.

item.title: 'You're paid to make that decision' – Eriksson's midfield dilemma
item.description: Former players and coaching staff from England's 2006 'Golden Generation' reflect on how manager Sven-Goran Eriksson struggled to get the best out of Steven Gerrard and Frank Lampard in a star-studded midfield.

item.title: Olise scores hat-trick as France beat Northern Ireland
item.description: Bayern Munich's Michael Olise scores a hat-trick in France's 3-1 victory over Northern Ireland in their final World Cup warm-up match in Lille.

item.title: Norway dress up as Vikings for World Cup
item.description: Watch behind-the-scenes footage as the Norway squad dress up as Vikings for a team photo before the World Cup.

item.title: Scotland score four in first half to beat Bolivia
item.description: Watch the goals as Scotland score four in the first half to beat Bolivia in their final World Cup warm-up.

item.title: Highlights: Romania 2-1 Wales
item.description: Watch the best of the action as Romania defeat Wales 2-1 in Bucharest.

item.title: McGinn in the form of his life and now dancing to Scotland's tune
item.description: If Scotland hit the mark in their upcoming matches the way John McGinn does in front of the media then this could be a special World Cup for Steve Clarke's side, writes Tom English.

item.title: Crespo on Messi, returning to England and life as a manager
item.description: Goals, passion and intensity define Hernan Crespo as a player. They are the qualities he wants his teams to show as a coach.

item.title: Billionaire West Ham co-owner accused of abusing his power and preying on women for sex
item.description: Women say David Sullivan told them they needed to have sex with him to feature in his newspapers.

item.title: Lionesses need setbacks like Spain loss - Wiegman
item.description: Sarina Wiegman says her "hurt" England side need setbacks like their humbling defeat by Spain in order to improve.

item.title: Palace hold initial talks with Lens manager Sage
item.description: Crystal Palace hold talks with Lens manager Pierre Sage about becoming their new head coach.

item.title: 'The things that forge them are similar' - the origins of Messi & Ronaldo
item.description: Both from humble beginnings, BBC Sport looks at how Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo became two of the greatest footballers of all time.

item.title: Watch: Weir hat-trick as Scotland thump Israel
item.description: Watch as Scotland thump Israel 6-0 in World Cup qualifying Group B4, with captain Caroline Weir scoring a hat-trick.

item.title: Highlights: Wales held to draw in Montenegro
item.description: Watch highlights as Montenegro and Wales draw in Podgorica in Women's World Cup qualifying.

item.title: Watch: When Hagi's Romania thrashed Wales
item.description: Wales travel to Bucharest to face Romania on Saturday for the first time since their World Cup qualifying defeat in 1992.

item.title: Messi or Ronaldo - who is the greatest of all time?
item.description: Some of the greats of the game debate the long-asked question: Lionel Messi or Cristiano Ronaldo?

item.title: Bellamy urges Wales to dominate Romania
item.description: Head coach Craig Bellamy wants Wales team to try to dominate in Saturday's friendly against Romania in Bucharest.

item.title: Watch 24 Hours with Ella Toone on BBC iPlayer
item.description: Watch the trailer for a new BBC Sport documentary offering unprecedented behind-the-scenes access to Manchester United and England star Ella Toone as she prepares for her wedding, while still coming to terms with the loss of her father.

item.title: Shearer on Beckham, pressure and Argentina heartbreak
item.description: Alan Shearer reflects on his and England's performances at the 1998 World Cup.

item.title: How can athletes play with ICDs, and what happens when one goes off?
item.description: BBC Sport explores how athletes with cardiac health issues are able to continue playing, the risks, and what happens when an ICD goes off.

item.title: Netherlands' Timber out of World Cup with injury
item.description: Arsenal defender Jurrien Timber is ruled out of the Netherlands' World Cup campaign with a groin injury, while Brazil's Neymar is making good progress from injury.

item.title: Norway boss brands Scotland's Clarke 'unprofessional'
item.description: Steve Clarke is branded "unprofessional" by Norway counterpart Stale Solbakken after Scotland cancelled a training-ground friendly before the World Cup.

item.title: Rashford 'dead cert to start for me' - Walcott
item.description: Former England winger Theo Walcott is "most psyched" about Marcus Rashford for the Three Lions at the World Cup.

item.title: Rowing boats & regrets - Scotland's undefeated 1974 World Cup campaign
item.description: Joe Jordan and Davie Hay remember Scotland's 1974 World Cup campaign, that ended at the group stage despite an unbeaten record.

item.title: O'Neill praises 'fantastic' departing Deschamps
item.description: Northern Ireland boss Michael O'Neill praises outgoing French boss Didier Deschamps as a "fantastic national team manager".

item.title: Inside the fairytale journey of Scotland captain Robertson
item.description: Andy Robertson has come a long way since playing amateur football. Now he is edging towards become Scotland's most capped men's player.

item.title: How Emslie went from giving birth to World Cup qualifiers inside six months
item.description: Scotland's Claire Emslie was back playing competitive football just five months after giving birth and is now targeting next year's World Cup finals in Brazil.

item.title: Ex-England midfielder Dyer named new Southend boss
item.description: Southend United appoint ex-Ipswich, Newcastle and England midfielder Kieron Dyer as their new head coach on an initial two-year contract.

item.title: Mourinho set for Real after Perez re-elected
item.description: Jose Mourinho is set to confirmed as Real Madrid's new head coach after Florentino Perez was re-elected as the club's president until 2030.

item.title: FAI tight-lipped on venue switch for Israel game
item.description: The Football Association of Ireland [FAI] says it will "continue to meet to discuss the operational aspects" of hosting Israel in the Nations League.

item.title: From dazzling winger to veteran who barely runs - the evolution of Messi
item.description: Lionel Messi is set for his sixth World Cup with Argentina. Guillem Balague looks at how the Argentine has evolved.

item.title: Wilkinson reveals Wales plane was hit by lightning
item.description: Boss Rhian Wilkinson says Wales are happy to be at home in Tuesday's Women's World Cup qualifier against Czech Republic after revealing their team plane was struck by lightning en route to Montenegro.

item.title: Are Portugal better without Ronaldo - and is he undroppable?
item.description: As Cristiano Ronaldo prepares for his sixth World Cup with Portugal, BBC Sport take a closer look at his place in the team.

item.title: The pioneer in an unlikely World Cup team
item.description: When Desmond Armstrong faced the media at the World Cup in 1990, the first question he got was one that would stick in his mind for the wrong reasons.

item.title: Messi v Ronaldo: A rivalry that's dominated for two decades
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi have been at the top of the game for the past 20 years, BBC Sport looks at a rivalry that has shaped football.

item.title: The anatomy of a Golden Boot winner - and who could win it in 2026?
item.description: A look at the common attributes of World Cup Golden Boot winners and the claims of the leading 2026 candidates examined.

item.title: Wars, sanctions and punishments: Iraq's 40-year World Cup wait
item.description: Iraq return to the World Cup with ambitions of delivering stronger performances and results than first time round in 1986.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: Tottenham remain keen on Hearts' Wilson - gossip
item.description: Hearts forward remains in thoughts of Tottenham as Hibs attacker is due back for pre-season.

item.title: WSL 2 side Durham face threat of closure
item.description: Women's Super League 2 side Durham warn "the club will have to cease operations at all levels" unless a buyer can be found in three weeks.

item.title: Beating France would be one of football's biggest stories - Ward
item.description: Republic of Ireland boss Carla Ward says beating France to qualify automatically for the 2027 Women's World Cup would "be one of the biggest stories in world football".

item.title: NI squad has exciting future ahead - McKenna
item.description: Northern Ireland captain Rebecca McKenna says new manager Michael McArdle has "brought a breath of fresh air" since taking over at the helm.

item.title: Wales' Hughes leaves promoted Crystal Palace
item.description: Wales forward Elise Hughes leaves Crystal Palace, a little more than a month after helping them return to the Women's Super League.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.
